Purpose & context
Kushal is building My Blazor Magazine, a free open-source static blog and UI showcase site deployed to GitHub Pages. The stack is Blazor WebAssembly (.NET 10), a custom markdown content processing pipeline, and a companion Cloudflare Workers API (Hono + TypeScript) for analytics, reactions, view counts, and a passkey-secured board. The project targets .NET, C#, and ASP.NET web developers as its primary audience, with a secondary goal of accessibility for non-technical readers. Kushal publishes under the author identity myblazor-team and collaborates with Claude as a core part of the content and development workflow.
Current state
The project is mature and actively maintained. Recent work spans:

Content pipeline: Long-form technical articles on SOLID principles (all five, plus a DIP-focused opinion piece), SQL Server, PostgreSQL/Npgsql, TypeScript, Avalonia UI, Git/trunk-based development, cloud AI fallback architectures, QA in AI-accelerated development, and a 2007 Toyota Camry owner's guide.
Infrastructure: CI/CD via GitHub Actions with scheduled publishing, draft post support, PR artifact previews, audio generation (KittenTTS Python + ffmpeg → MP3), LibMan for local highlight.js, and Node.js 24 action compatibility.
Features: Multi-theme system (Light, Dark, Sepia, Solarized Light, Solarized Dark, High Contrast, System), TTS audio player, author profiles system, tag filtering, Showcase CRUD catalog with localStorage persistence, MasterDetail component, responsive navigation, and OpenTelemetry integration.
Resilience principle: Cloudflare Workers is an optional dependency; all backend-dependent features degrade gracefully when offline.

On the horizon

Continued long-form article production across technical topics
Ongoing CSS/accessibility refinement across themes
Potential further Showcase and component enhancements

Key learnings & principles

Author ID convention is critical: author: myblazor-team (hyphenated ID), never the display name "Observer Team" — mismatches cause build warnings and broken author resolution.
featured field: Omit entirely from non-featured posts; never write featured: false. Only include featured: true when explicitly promoting.
draft: true: Omit unless explicitly requested.
Tags: Lowercase and hyphenated (e.g., design-principles, deep-dive).
File naming: content/blog/YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md
Article delivery: Always complete, never truncated — Kushal has explicitly flagged prior AI tools cutting off long-form content as a pain point.
Article length target: 5,000–15,000+ words for deep-dive pieces; exhaustive and patient in tone.
Research before writing: Web searches for version numbers, release dates, and historical facts must be completed before drafting — never include unverified specifics.
CSS blast radius: Scope styles tightly to component-level .razor.css files; minimize global CSS impact.
Proactive defect finding: Kushal expects Claude to identify and fix all similar issues in scope, not just the one explicitly reported.
Full file output: Always deliver complete corrected files, not diffs or partial snippets.

Approach & patterns

Project knowledge first: Before writing any article or making code changes, search project knowledge (dump.txt) to confirm front matter schema, file naming, and conventions — two parallel queries work well (one broad structural, one schema-specific).
Thematic research batches: Run all web searches upfront by topic cluster before drafting; interleaving search and writing degrades coherence.
Article structure: Numbered parts, mandatory code examples (C# / ASP.NET Core primary, others as needed), anecdotes and analogies for accessibility, practical recommendations, closing resources section.
Build verification sequence: dotnet format → dotnet restore → content processor → dotnet test → dotnet list package → bash export.sh
Content processor: Reads from content/blog/ and content/authors/, outputs to src/ObserverMagazine.Web/wwwroot/; skips drafts and future-dated posts relative to UTC now.
Audio pipeline: KittenTTS Python (nano model, Python 3.12, not 3.13) + ffmpeg → 64kbps mono MP3 → wwwroot/blog-data/{slug}.mp3; model cached in Actions; audio not committed to git.
Communication style: Kushal is direct and terse; expects Claude to act without hand-holding and deliver results, not explanations of what to do.

Tools & resources

Stack: Blazor WASM, .NET 10, ASP.NET Core, xUnit, bUnit, highlight.js (via LibMan), KittenTTS, ffmpeg, Hono (Cloudflare Workers), TypeScript
CI/CD: GitHub Actions (deploy.yml, pr-check.yml); GitHub Pages
Project knowledge: dump.txt — full codebase export generated by a bash script (excludes docs/ folder); used as primary context source for all sessions
Content tools: Custom ObserverMagazine.ContentProcessor CLI; generate_audio.py with preprocess_programming_terms() for TTS text normalization (handles .NET, C#, F#, C++, ASP.NET, file extensions, operator symbols, etc.)
Reusable prompt template: Exists in project knowledge for generating consistent long-form articles
